citations.narrative:
In the indoor portion of the enclosure housing one adult male and one adult female common marmosets, there is a
baseboard along the floor and wall that is warped, creating a space that has an accumulation of dried, brown material that
is difficult to clean.
Surfaces that do not readily facilitate cleaning and accumulate dirt and debris can harbor microbes and attract vermin that
can cause animal illness. The facility must ensure that the surfaces of housing facilities are constructed in a manner and
made of materials that allow them to be readily cleaned and sanitized, or removed and replaced when worn or soiled.
TO BE CORRECTED BY 05/27/2026

There is an approximately 4'X2' area that is torn open in one section of the perimeter fence; the metal is pulled away
along one edge, creating a gap. The facility representative told the inspector she was unaware that the fence was
damaged.
Perimeter fences that are damaged cannot serve as secondary containment in the event of animal escape, nor as a
barrier to prevent the entry of other animals or unauthorized persons. The facility must assess all perimeter fencing at the
facility for structural integrity and height and make repairs or replacements as needed to ensure all parts of the perimeter
fence are sturdy, of the minimum required height, and a minimum of 3 feet away from primary enclosures. The perimeter
fence must protect the animals by restricting entry of outside animals and unauthorized persons from going through it, or
under it or having contact with the animals at the facility.
This is a 1st Re-License Inspection for a Class “C” license. The inspection and exit briefing were conducted with the
applicant.
All items must be in compliance within 2 more inspections or by 07/12/2026 or the applicant will forfeit the license fee and
must wait 6 months to reapply. Please contact your inspector to schedule your next Re-License Inspection.
Regulated activity can continue under your existing USDA license until it expires.
n

narrative:
3.75(c)(1) 
   
Housing facilities, general. 
In the indoor portion of the enclosure housing one adult male and one adult female common marmosets, there is a 
baseboard along the floor and wall that is warped, creating a space that has an accumulation of dried, brown material that 
is difficult to clean. 
Surfaces that do not readily facilitate cleaning and accumulate dirt and debris can harbor microbes and attract vermin that 
can cause animal illness. The facility must ensure that the surfaces of housing facilities are constructed in a manner and 
made of materials that allow them to be readily cleaned and sanitized, or removed and replaced when worn or soiled. 
TO BE CORRECTED BY 05/27/2026 
3.127(d)  Repeat
   
Facilities, outdoor. 
There is an approximately 4'X2' area that is torn open in one section of the perimeter fence; the metal is pulled away 
along one edge, creating a gap. The facility representative told the inspector she was unaware that the fence was 
damaged. 
Perimeter fences that are damaged cannot serve as secondary containment in the event of animal escape, nor as a 
barrier to prevent the entry of other animals or unauthorized persons. The facility must assess all perimeter fencing at the 
facility for structural integrity and height and make repairs or replacements as needed to ensure all parts of the perimeter 
fence are sturdy, of the minimum required height, and a minimum of 3 feet away from primary enclosures. The perimeter 
fence must protect the animals by restricting entry of outside animals and unauthorized persons from going through it, or 
under it or having contact with the animals at the facility. 
 
This is a 1st Re-License Inspection for a Class “C” license. The inspection and exit briefing were conducted with the 
applicant. 
 
All items must be in compliance within 2 more inspections or by 07/12/2026 or the applicant will forfeit the license fee and 
must wait 6 months to reapply. Please contact your inspector to schedule your next Re-License Inspection. 
 
Regulated activity can continue under your existing USDA license until it expires.
